rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits>=1 -> FAIL

results.reason: Source-domain biology paper on bristlemouth eye-reference photophores; provides the analogy itself, not an LLM functional equivalent, and only one content-word substring ('counterillumination') matches.
General Wikipedia article on biological counter-illumination camouflage; no LLM relevance and no full content-word substring match.
Detection-side method using KL across rewrites as a feature; opposite direction (detect rather than cancel) and well below all thresholds.
Self-Disguise Attack trains an LLM to disguise its own output to evade AI-text detection — same functional effect as ECED's counter-emission, exceeding sem and func thresholds.
Humanizing the Machine uses a closed-loop humanizing proxy decoder that overrides predictions in real time to evade detection — directly the ECED real-time counter-emission mechanism.
Prompt-guided evasion of detectors; relevant direction but lacks live-reference closed-loop cancellation, all scores below 0.7.
Defender-side rewrite training to amplify human/AI disparity; opposite functional role and below all thresholds.
OOD detection treating human text as outliers — detection direction, no functional overlap with closed-loop ambient cancellation.

fail_reason: Two prior works achieve the same functional effect: rank 4 (Self-Disguise Attack, sem 0.78 / func 0.82) trains an LLM to disguise its output to evade detection, and rank 5 (Humanizing the Machine, sem 0.80 / func 0.85) uses a closed-loop humanizing proxy decoder overriding predictions in real time — both replicate ECED's counter-emission evasion mechanism.
